architecture.summary: The paper proposes Linear Task Vector (LTV), a method for compressing in-context learning demonstrations into compact hidden-state representations. LTV uses a closed-form linear mapping that estimates demonstration effects through regression to minimize the discrepancy between task vector-based and ICL-based inference. The mechanism operates by extracting task vectors from LLM hidden states and applying a learned linear transformation to align the predictive distribution (next-token probabilities) with full ICL.
trained_components: linear mapping for task vector extraction

biblio.title: Distributional Alignment as a Criterion for Designing Task Vectors in In-Context Learning

claims.statement: LTV consistently outperforms existing task vector baselines, improving average accuracy by 9.2%
d_NTP serves as a performance proxy, exhibiting a strong negative correlation with downstream accuracy
task vectors from a larger model can enhance a smaller model's performance by 6.4%
LTV outperforms the baselines on regression tasks
Inference using task vectors should align their predictive distribution with that of ICL
